Title: Genome-wide analysis of TALE superfamily in Triticum aestivum reveals TaKNOX11-A is involved in abiotic stress response

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Functional validation of TaKNOX11-A was performed in a heterologous Arabidopsis system; its precise downstream target genes and whether its role in abiotic stress tolerance in wheat is mediated through ABA or MeJA signaling remain unresolved. Protein–protein interactions with BLH1-like proteins and involvement in ABA-related pathways are suggested by STRING predictions and analogies to Arabidopsis KNAT3/BLH1 but were not experimentally validated in this study.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaKNOX11-A is a wheat KNOXII-type TALE transcription factor with strong expression across developmental stages and high transcriptional induction by drought, salt, MeJA, and ABA treatments.
2. qRT-PCR shows that TaKNOX11-A expression peaks at 6 h of drought, 9 h of salt, and 1 h of MeJA and ABA treatment in wheat seedlings, consistent with promoter enrichment for stress- and hormone-responsive cis-elements.
3. Overexpression of TaKNOX11-A in Arabidopsis increases seed germination rates, root length, and fresh weight under PEG6000- and NaCl-induced stress compared with WT and knat3 plants.
4. Under soil drought and salt treatments, TaKNOX11-A-overexpressing Arabidopsis lines display higher survival rates, higher proline content, and lower malondialdehyde levels than WT and knat3, indicating reduced cellular damage.
5. These results support that TaKNOX11-A functions as a positive regulator of plant drought and salt tolerance and represents a useful genetic resource for improving wheat stress resistance.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression data for 70 wheat TALE genes, including TaKNOX11-A, across nine developmental stages/tissues and under drought and heat stresses were obtained from the Wheat Expression Browser (Chinese Spring; TPM values summarized in Additional file 14: Table S14).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of wheat TaKNOX11-A in Arabidopsis enhances drought and salt tolerance, leading to higher survival, stress-resistant growth, and altered stress-related physiological indices under drought (PEG6000 or water withholding) and salt (NaCl) stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TALE superfamily transcription factor; KNOX (KNOTTED-like homeodomain) protein of the KNOXII subclass
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (predicted for KNOX subfamily proteins, including TaKNOX11-A)
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Among KNOXII members, TaKNOX11-A shows one of the highest transcription levels across wheat developmental stages and tissues. qRT-PCR in wheat seedlings shows that TaKNOX11-A is strongly induced by drought, salt, MeJA, and ABA treatments, with peak expression at 6 h under drought, 9 h under salt, and 1 h under MeJA and ABA.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (PEG6000 in Arabidopsis; dehydration of wheat seedlings on dry filter paper; soil dry-down), salt stress (NaCl), heat stress (42 °C for wheat seedlings), and hormone treatments with ABA, MeJA, ETH, and GA.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response; drought and salt tolerance in plants
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 8–10% PEG6000 and 100–150 mM NaCl, TaKNOX11-A overexpression lines show significantly higher seed germination rates than WT and knat3 (p<0.05); under drought and salt stress, overexpression lines have significantly longer roots and higher fresh weight than WT and knat3 (p<0.05); after soil drought or salt treatments and rewatering, overexpression lines display significantly higher survival rates and significantly higher proline but lower malondialdehyde contents than WT and knat3 (p<0.05).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Overexpression of TaKNOX11-A in Arabidopsis increases seed germination rates under PEG6000 and NaCl treatments and results in longer roots and greater fresh weight under drought and salt stress compared with WT and knat3 plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wheat seedlings of cultivar Chinese Spring for hormone and stress treatments; Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 background plants; TaKNOX11-A-overexpressing Arabidopsis T3 lines (OE1, OE2, OE3); knat3 mutant Arabidopsis line; Agrobacterium tumefaciens GV3101 carrying pCAMBIA1302-TaKNOX11-A.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Identification of TALE genes by HMMER3 using TALE domain PF00046 and domain confirmation via SMART and NCBI CD-search; phylogenetic and motif analysis (MEGA7, MEME); promoter cis-element analysis with PlantCARE; RNA-seq-based expression profiling from the wheat expression browser across tissues and under drought/heat; qRT-PCR of TaKNOX11-A and other TALE genes under drought, salt, MeJA, and ABA in wheat; cloning of TaKNOX11-A CDS into pCAMBIA1302 and Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of Arabidopsis Col-0; selection of T3 homozygous overexpression lines; use of knat3 mutant as comparison; seed germination assays on MS medium supplemented with PEG6000 or NaCl; root length and fresh weight measurements under PEG6000 and NaCl; soil drought and salt stress assays followed by rewatering and survival assessment; measurement of proline and malondialdehyde contents in Arabidopsis leaves.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression of TaKNOX11-A in Arabidopsis Col-0 and phenotyping under drought and salt stress (seed germination assays on PEG6000 and NaCl media; root length and fresh weight measurements; soil drought and salt tolerance assays with survival scoring), combined with qRT-PCR analysis of TaKNOX11-A expression in wheat under abiotic and hormone treatments and measurement of proline and malondialdehyde contents.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Genome-wide HMM search for TALE (PF00046) domains in the wheat genome to identify TALE genes, followed by selection of candidates with many stress- and hormone-related cis-elements and strong stress-induced expression, cloning of the TaKNOX11-A CDS into the pCAMBIA1302 overexpression vector, and Agrobacterium-mediated transformation into Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: TaKNOX11-A acts as a positive regulator of drought and salt tolerance when expressed in plants and is strongly inducible by multiple abiotic and hormonal signals in wheat, indicating that it is a promising genetic resource for improving wheat stress resistance. The gene could be used in transgenic or genome-editing approaches to enhance drought and salt tolerance and as a candidate locus for developing stress-resilient wheat varieties.
